---
title: "Lecture: Triangulating Traces for Humanitarian Response"
subtitle: "08:30 – 10:00 · May 19, 2026 · SPACERAISE @ GSSI"
author: "Elisabetta Pietrostefani, University of Liverpool"
date: "2026-05-19"
---

```{=html}
<div class="part-badge">Lecture · 90 min</div>
```

::: objectives
**Learning objectives**

By the end of this lecture you will be able to:

-   Define what we mean by *triangulated data methods* in the context of disaster research
-   Contrast traditional and non-traditional (digital) data sources and their trade-offs
-   Explain how satellite remote sensing is used to quantify physical damage
-   Understand how survey and administrative data complement earth observation
:::

## References

*References to be added.*
